data.title: Everybody Loves Languages to go private via cash business combination
data.description:
• Everybody Loves Languages (ELL:CA) entered into a business combination agreement with ELL Ventures dated December 24, 2025.
  • The transaction provides cash consideration of $0.085/share to all ELLC shareholders other than ELL Ventures.
  • The agreement was approved by ELLC’s board on December 23, 2025 following a recommendation from its independent committee.
  • ELLC and ELL Ventures will amalgamate into a new entity, with minority shareholders receiving redeemable preferred shares immediately redeemed for cash.
  • ELL Ventures shareholders will receive equivalent shares in the new amalgamated company with similar rights.
  • Following completion, ELLC plans to delist from the TSX Venture Exchange and become a privately held company.
  • The transaction is expected to close on or about March 10, 2026.
  • ELL Ventures is controlled by ELLC’s CEO Gali Bar-Ziv and CFO Khurram Qureshi, who together own about 10% of ELLC shares and will roll their interests into ELL Ventures prior to closing.

data.title: XTL Biopharmaceuticals receives Nasdaq bid price deficiency notice
data.description:
• XTL Biopharmaceuticals (XTLB) received a Nasdaq notice dated December 22, 2025, for failing to maintain the $1 minimum bid price requirement.
  • The deficiency is based on the company’s ADS closing bid price being below $1 for 30 consecutive business days.
  • XTL has a 180-day compliance period, ending June 22, 2026, to regain compliance.

data.title: UnitedHealth ends seven straight sessions of losses
data.description:
UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH) closed in the green after seven consecutive sessions of losses, as the company’s shares closed 0.86% higher at $327.58 on Wednesday.

In the preceding seven sessions, the company lost over 4.9%, compared to a rise of 1.2% in the S&P 500 Index. Similarly, shares of the company have gone down by over 35.2% compared to a gain of 17.5% in the broader S&P 500 Index in 2025.

According to Seeking Alpha analyst Envision Research, UNH has worked through recent headwinds, with profits recovering and a foundation in place for renewed growth. With a forward P/E of 20.5x and potential for double-digit return on investment, Envision Research believes UNH offers an attractive risk/reward profile.

Analyst Pierre Axel Gide suggests the company is well-positioned despite two difficult years of cyber incidents, leadership turnover, and regulatory investigations. The company’s scale and diversified healthcare operations form a durable competitive moat that supports the long-term investment.

However, according to KM Capital, UNH faces elevated uncertainty due to potential changes in ACA subsidies and major company repositioning plans for 2026. Due to persistent uncertainties and fair valuation, it downgraded UNH to Hold.

Looking at Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating, UNH has a Hold rating with a score of 3.21 out of 5. The company was rated A+ for profitability, while it got an F for growth and a B- for valuation.

However, Wall Street analysts appeared bullish, as 19 rated the stock a Buy or above, 6 rated it a Hold, and 2 considered it a Sell or lower.

Similarly, Seeking Alpha analysts also issued a Buy call for UNH with a score of 3.84 out of 5.

data.title: Prudential Financial shares rise after six-session losing streak
data.description:
Prudential Financial (PRU) shares snapped six straight sessions of losses, as the stock was up 0.3% at $114.55 on Wednesday.

The life and health insurance company fell 2.5% in the preceding six sessions. The stock has fallen nearly 4% so far this year, compared to an 18% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index (SP500).

PRU is up 7% over the past one month.

Most recently, Prudential Financial (PRU) announced an up to $1B share repurchase program, effective January 1, 2026, to December 31, 2026.

"Given PRU's strong business profile and the Notes' structural features, these securities may suit investors prioritizing income stability and capital preservation," pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, PRU has a Hold rating with a score of 3.4 out of 5. The company received B in the prospect of profitability, while it got D+ growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, two analysts gave PRU a Strong Buy rating. 14 analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while one recommended Strong Sell.

 

Seeking Alpha analysts are positive and see the stock as a Buy.

data.title: Ryder System is the top performing cargo transportation stocks YTD
data.description:
The S&P 500 industrials sector has delivered a robust performance in 2025, with a year to date performance of up +18.2%.

The sector ranked as the third-best-performing sector year to date and, outpacing the broader benchmark S&P 500 (SP500).

As we look at the sub-sectors within the industrials sector, below is a list of the top 10 cargo transportation stocks arranged by their YTD performance. The list includes market capitalization data and Quant ratings for each stock.

The list is topped by Ryder System, with a YTD performance of +25.59%. J.B. Hunt Transport Services (JBHT) and XPO (XPO) are next, with Full Truck Alliance (YMM) and Knight-Swift Transportation (KNX) rounding out the rest of the top five.

Most stocks on the list carry a Quant rating of “Hold.” Notably, U-Haul Holding (UHAL) is the only stock with a “Sell” Quant rating of 1.54 and is among the weakest performers with a YTD decline of -25.88%.

Here is the list:

  1. Ryder System, YTD perf: +25.59%, Quant Rating: Hold 2.81

  2. J.B. Hunt Transport Services (JBHT), YTD perf: +15.45%, Quant Rating: Hold 3.41

  3. XPO (XPO), YTD perf: +6.82%, Quant Rating: Hold 2.82

data.title: Is the stock market open on Christmas?
data.description:
Season's Greetings and best wishes to all of our readership as we go into the holidays!

Investors will get a respite from the stock market on Christmas Day, with both equity index futures and regular trading shut. The trading of metals and energy futures on Comex and the New York Mercantile Exchange will also be closed.

Government bond and corporate debt markets will be shut as well, along with the majority of banks and credit unions. For those searching for something to trade, crypto will be available all day, with Bitcoin (BTC-USD) last +0.09% at $87,530.63.

Ho, Ho, Ho! 

With the S&P 500 (SP500) headed for another year of double-digit gains, market participants are now looking out for the so-called Santa Claus rally - a historical trend that has seen the benchmark index post gains during the last five trading days of the year and the first two of the next year. The rally is off to a good start after the gauge gained +0.32% to end at a record level of 6,932.05 points in the truncated Christmas Eve trading session. 

The Santa indicator was first recorded in 1972 by Wall Street legend Yale Hirsch, the creator of Stock Trader's Almanac. He famously declared, "If Santa Claus should fail to call, bears may come to Broad and Wall."

While Yale passed away in 2021, the Stock Trader's Almanac is now produced by his son Jeff, who noted the importance of taking the "Santa Claus Rally" into consideration along with the "First Five Days" early warning system (how stocks perform in the first five sessions of the year) and the "January Barometer" (S&P 500 performance in the first month of the year) to know what to expect in 2026.

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the S&P 500: (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), and (SPXU). 

Don't forget to check out Seeking Alpha's comprehensive guide to Stock Market Holidays.

data.title: Rio Tinto snaps seven straight sessions of gains
data.description:
Rio Tinto (RIO) shares snapped seven straight sessions of gains, as the stock closed marginally lower at $80.8 on Wednesday.

The iron ore supplier gained about 7% in the preceding seven sessions. Overall, the stock rose over 37% so far this year, compared to the 17% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

RIO is up 15% over the past one month. The stock closed 1% high on Tuesday at $80.97.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, RIO has a Hold rating with a score of 3.47 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, while it got D+ in the growth factor.

However, Wall Street and Seeking Alpha analysts are bullish on the stock.

Turning to the Wall Street community, five analysts gave RIO a Buy and above, three analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, and no one recommended Sell or lower. Seeking Alpha analysts also see the stock as a Buy.

Earlier in the month, new CEO Simon Trott outlined a plan to cut costs and sell assets in an effort to make the business "stronger, sharper and simpler" by delivering $5 billion-$10 billion in cash proceeds from its asset base by divesting, selling minority stakes, cutting roles, and restructuring some existing financing.

A recent Seeking Alpha analyst downgraded the stock to Hold noting that “RIO's production growth and project expansions are offset by flat cash flow due to higher capex and volatile commodity prices, with increased net debt adding near-term pressure.”

However, the analysis added that long-term prospects for the stock remain strong due to lithium expansion and global demand.

data.title: REV is the top construction machinery stock in terms of YTD performance

Seeking Alpha took a look at the industrials subsector that covers companies providing construction machinery and heavy transportation equipment and identified the top YTD performers. REV (REVG) grabbed the number one spot with a solid +95.64% gain.
